summary: The statement that all intrinsic laryngeal muscles are exclusively supplied by the recurrent laryngeal nerve is false due to specific muscular differences such as those involving the cricothyroid and certain aspects of the cricoarytenoids' innervation patterns. To accurately describe this complex structure requires acknowledging individual variances among different laryngeal musculature regarding neural supply sources. Agent2 concluded after analyzing multiple medical references that while most intrinsically important nerves serve many parts of the larynx via the RLN, some critical distinctions exist particularly concerning how various subgroups within it get innervated differently – notably highlighting unique roles played especially around the cricothyroid and related structures which do not strictly adhere
